---
title: "INFO 4490 Project 1: Exploratory Analysis"
authors: Zhalae Daneshvari, Nick Johnson, Jolene Le
---

# Objective(s)

State the question(s) you are answering or the problem(s) you are solving clearly.

# Data collection and cleaning

Have an initial draft of your data cleaning appendix. Document every step that takes your raw data file(s) and turns it into the analysis-ready data set that you would submit with your final project. Include text narrative describing your data collection (downloading, scraping, surveys, etc) and any additional data curation/cleaning (merging data frames, filtering, transformations of variables, etc). Include code for data curation/cleaning, but not collection.

Data was pulled from https://wwwn.cdc.gov/nchs/nhanes/search/datapage.aspx?Component=Questionnaire&Cycle=2021-2023 that contains individual xpt documents for each of the following labratory tests, examinations, and questionaires. Here are the specific features we decided to use: 

Weight history
SEQN - Respondent sequence number
WHD010 - Current self-reported height (inches)
WHD020 - Current self-reported weight (pounds)
WHD050 - Self-reported weight - 1 yr ago (pounds)
WHQ070 - Tried to lose weight in past year

Reproductive Health
RHQ031 - Had regular periods in past 12 months
RHQ131 - Ever been pregnant?
Sleep Disorders
SLD012 - Sleep hours - weekdays or workdays

Cigarette Use
SMQ020 - Smoked at least 100 cigarettes in life
SMQ040 - Do you now smoke cigarettes?

Physical Activity
PAD680 - Minutes sedentary activity

Mental Health - Depression Screener
DPQ050 - Poor appetite or overeating
DPQ020 - Feeling down, depressed, or hopeless

Medical Conditions
MCQ010 - Ever been told you have asthma
MCQ053 - Taking treatment for anemia/past 3 mos
MCQ160b - Ever told had congestive heart failure
MCQ160e - Ever told you had heart attack
MCQ160f - Ever told you had a stroke
MCQ160m - Ever told you had thyroid problem
MCQ160l - Ever told you had any liver condition
MCQ510a - Liver condition: Fatty liver
MCQ510b - Liver condition: Liver fibrosis
MCQ510c - Liver condition: Liver cirrhosis
MCQ510d - Liver condition: Viral hepatitis
MCQ510e - Liver condition: Autoimmune hepatitis

Kidney Conditions - Urology
KIQ022 - Ever told you had weak/failing kidneys?

Body Measures
BMXBMI - Body MAss Index (kg/m**2)

Diabetes
DIQ010 - Doctor told you have diabetes (DROP participants that have diabetes)

Blood Pressure and Cholesterol
BPQ020 - Ever told you had high blood pressure
BPQ080 - Doctor told you - high cholesterol level

Alcohol Use
ALQ130 - Avg # alcoholic drinks/day/past 12 mos

Demographics
RIAGENDR - Gender
RIDAGEYR - Age in years at screening
RIDRETH3 - Race/Hispanic origin w/ NH Asian

Target Column - Plasma Fasting Glucose
LBXGLU - Fasting Glucose (mg/dL)
Target Column - Glycohemoglobin
LBXGH - Glycohemoglobin (%)

Our collection process was as follows: 

1) Read and Standardize: Load each xpt file, keeping SEQN as key to join files

2) Merge Fasting Glucose and Glycohemoglobin (GHB/GLU targets), Diabetes Questionaire (exclusions), and all other chosen survey questions and demographics.

3) Filter: 
Drop rows of child respondents. (RIDAGEYR < 18).
Drop rows without Fasting Glucose and Glycohemoglobin (GHB/GLU) present.
Drop rows of respondents who are diagnosed with diabetes as we want to predict if someone is prediabetic from the set of respondents who do not already have diabetes.

4) Rename variables to clear, readable names (e.g. PAD680: sedentary_minutes)
Add labeled answer columns for many yes/no or categorical items (e.g. RIAGENDR=1.0: sex=male)

5) Enforce numeric datatypes for numeric columns and set values outside of plausible ranges to NA. Ranges are as follows: 1) "age_years": (18, 99), "bmi": (10, 80), "A1c": (3, 20),"FPG": (50, 500),"sleep_hours_weekdays": (0, 24),"sedentary_minutes": (0, 1440),"drinks_per_day_12mo": (0, 50),"height_in": (40, 84),"weight_lb": (60, 700),"weight_1y_ago_lb": (60, 700).

6) Compute Self-Reported BMI feature where BMI = (self reported weight (kg))/(height (m)^2)

7) Compute Change in weight feature where change in weight =  current weight minus self-reported weight 1 year ago. This will be positive if respondent gained weight and negative if they've lost weight.

8) Map response to survey question: Have you ever smoked 100+ cigarettes? to: 1 if they responded "Yes", 0 if they responded "No" or Nan if respondent refused, answered "Don't know" or if response is missing. 

9) Map response to survey question: Do you smoke now? to 1 if they responded "Every day" or "Some days", 0 if they responded "Not at all", and Nan if they responded "Refused", "Don't know", or did not respond.

10) Drop columns that wont be used in the model such as weight, cycle, and ever_pregnant columns.

11) Drop Fasting Glucose and Glycohemoglobin Columns derived from lab tests that were used to construct target prediabetes column. 

12) Drop columns used to remove respondents who are already diabetic.



```{python}
import os, warnings
from pathlib import Path
import pandas as pd, numpy as np

# Settings
NHANES_DIR = "data-raw/nhanes_xpt"
OUT_CSV = "data-raw/nhanes2123_prediabetes_features_labeled.csv"
CYCLES = ["L"]  # 2021–2023
ADULTS_ONLY = True  # keep age >= 18
EXCLUDE_DIAGNOSED = True  # drop diagnosed DM / on meds
REQUIRE_LAB_FOR_ROW = True  # require A1c or FPG present
REQUIRE_FASTING_FOR_WEIGHT = False  # use WTSAF2YR if True & available


# Helpers
def xpt_path(prefix, L):
    p1 = os.path.join(NHANES_DIR, f"{prefix}_{L}.XPT")
    p2 = os.path.join(NHANES_DIR, f"{prefix}_{L}.xpt")
    return p1 if os.path.exists(p1) else (p2 if os.path.exists(p2) else None)


def read_xpt(prefix, L):
    p = xpt_path(prefix, L)
    if not p:
        warnings.warn(f"Missing {prefix}_{L}.XPT; skipping.")
        return pd.DataFrame()
    df = pd.read_sas(p, format="xport")
    df.columns = [c.upper() for c in df.columns]
    if "SEQN" not in df.columns:
        warnings.warn(f"{prefix}_{L}: no SEQN; skipping.")
        return pd.DataFrame()
    return df


def safe_merge(
    left: pd.DataFrame, right: pd.DataFrame, src: str, key="SEQN"
) -> pd.DataFrame:
    """Left-merge on SEQN. If a non-key column collides, prefix it with SRC__."""
    if right.empty:
        return left
    r = right.copy()
    collisions = [c for c in r.columns if c != key and c in left.columns]
    if collisions:
        r.rename(columns={c: f"{src}__{c}" for c in collisions}, inplace=True)
    return left.merge(r, on=key, how="left", copy=False)


# Label Dictionsaries
YN = {1: "Yes", 2: "No", 7: "Refused", 9: "Don't know"}
SMOKE_NOW = {
    1: "Every day",
    2: "Some days",
    3: "Not at all",
    7: "Refused",
    9: "Don't know",
}
SEX_L = {1: "Male", 2: "Female"}
RIDRETH3_L = {
    1: "Mexican American",
    2: "Other Hispanic",
    3: "Non-Hispanic White",
    4: "Non-Hispanic Black",
    6: "Non-Hispanic Asian",
    7: "Other/Multiracial",
}

# Rename map
RENAME = {
    # Targets
    "LBXGH": "a1c_percent",  # A1c (%)
    "LBXGLU": "fpg_mg_dl",  # fasting plasma glucose (mg/dL)
    # Demographics / weight
    "RIDAGEYR": "age_years",
    "RIAGENDR": "sex_code",
    "RIDRETH3": "race_eth_code",
    "WTMEC2YR": "weight_sample",  # MEC sample weight (default)
    "WTSAF2YR": "weight_fasting",  # fasting subsample weight (from GLU)
    # Weight history (WHQ)
    "WHD010": "height_in",
    "WHD020": "weight_lb",
    "WHD050": "weight_1y_ago_lb",
    "WHQ070": "tried_lose_weight_past_year_code",
    # Reproductive (RHQ)
    "RHQ031": "regular_periods_12mo_code",
    "RHQ131": "ever_pregnant_code",
    # Sleep (SLQ)
    "SLD012": "sleep_hours_weekdays",
    # Cigarette use (SMQ)
    "SMQ020": "smoked_100_cigs_code",
    "SMQ040": "smoke_now_code",
    # Physical activity (PAD)
    "PAD680": "sedentary_minutes",
    # Depression (DPQ)
    "DPQ050": "dpq_poor_appetite_code",
    "DPQ020": "dpq_feeling_down_code",
    # Medical conditions (MCQ)
    "MCQ010": "ever_asthma_code",
    "MCQ053": "anemia_treatment_3mo_code",
    "MCQ160B": "ever_chf_code",
    "MCQ160E": "ever_heart_attack_code",
    "MCQ160F": "ever_stroke_code",
    "MCQ160M": "ever_thyroid_problem_code",
    "MCQ160L": "ever_liver_condition_code",
    "MCQ510A": "liver_fatty_code",
    "MCQ510B": "liver_fibrosis_code",
    "MCQ510C": "liver_cirrhosis_code",
    "MCQ510D": "liver_viral_hep_code",
    "MCQ510E": "liver_autoimmune_hep_code",
    # Kidney (KIQ)
    "KIQ022": "ever_weak_kidneys_code",
    # Body measures (BMX)
    "BMXBMI": "bmi",
    # Diabetes (DIQ) — for exclusions
    "DIQ010": "diq_diag_diabetes_code",
    "DIQ050": "diq_now_insulin_code",
    "DIQ070": "diq_now_pills_code",
    # BP & Cholesterol (BPQ)
    "BPQ020": "ever_high_bp_code",
    "BPQ080": "ever_high_chol_code",
    # Alcohol (ALQ)
    "ALQ130": "drinks_per_day_12mo",
}


# Build Cycle
def build_cycle(L: str) -> pd.DataFrame:
    # Required demographics/weights
    demo = read_xpt("DEMO", L)
    if demo.empty:
        raise SystemExit(f"DEMO_{L} is required (age/sex/race/weights).")

    # Targets
    ghb = read_xpt("GHB", L)  # A1c
    glu = read_xpt("GLU", L)  # FPG (+ WTSAF2YR)

    # Exclusions
    diq = read_xpt("DIQ", L)  # diagnosed DM / meds

    # Requested features
    whq = read_xpt("WHQ", L)
    rhq = read_xpt("RHQ", L)
    slq = read_xpt("SLQ", L)
    smq = read_xpt("SMQ", L)
    pad = read_xpt("PAQ", L)
    dpq = read_xpt("DPQ", L)
    mcq = read_xpt("MCQ", L)
    kiq = read_xpt("KIQ_U", L)
    bmx = read_xpt("BMX", L)
    bpq = read_xpt("BPQ", L)
    alq = read_xpt("ALQ", L)

    out = demo[["SEQN"]].copy()

    # Merge targets
    if not ghb.empty and "LBXGH" in ghb.columns:
        out = safe_merge(out, ghb[["SEQN", "LBXGH"]], "GHB")
    if not glu.empty:
        keep = [c for c in ["SEQN", "LBXGLU", "WTSAF2YR"] if c in glu.columns]
        out = safe_merge(out, glu[keep], "GLU")

    # Merge exclusions (DIQ)
    if not diq.empty:
        keep = [c for c in ["SEQN", "DIQ010", "DIQ050", "DIQ070"] if c in diq.columns]
        out = safe_merge(out, diq[keep], "DIQ")

    # Merge requested features (best-effort)
    if not whq.empty:
        out = safe_merge(
            out,
            whq[
                [
                    c
                    for c in ["SEQN", "WHD010", "WHD020", "WHD050", "WHQ070"]
                    if c in whq.columns
                ]
            ],
            "WHQ",
        )
    if not rhq.empty:
        out = safe_merge(
            out,
            rhq[[c for c in ["SEQN", "RHQ031", "RHQ131"] if c in rhq.columns]],
            "RHQ",
        )
    if not slq.empty:
        out = safe_merge(
            out, slq[[c for c in ["SEQN", "SLD012"] if c in slq.columns]], "SLQ"
        )
    if not smq.empty:
        out = safe_merge(
            out,
            smq[[c for c in ["SEQN", "SMQ020", "SMQ040"] if c in smq.columns]],
            "SMQ",
        )
    if not pad.empty:
        out = safe_merge(
            out, pad[[c for c in ["SEQN", "PAD680"] if c in pad.columns]], "PAD"
        )
    if not dpq.empty:
        out = safe_merge(
            out,
            dpq[[c for c in ["SEQN", "DPQ050", "DPQ020"] if c in dpq.columns]],
            "DPQ",
        )
    if not mcq.empty:
        keep = [
            c
            for c in [
                "SEQN",
                "MCQ010",
                "MCQ053",
                "MCQ160B",
                "MCQ160E",
                "MCQ160F",
                "MCQ160M",
                "MCQ160L",
                "MCQ510A",
                "MCQ510B",
                "MCQ510C",
                "MCQ510D",
                "MCQ510E",
            ]
            if c in mcq.columns
        ]
        out = safe_merge(out, mcq[keep], "MCQ")
    if not kiq.empty:
        out = safe_merge(
            out, kiq[[c for c in ["SEQN", "KIQ022"] if c in kiq.columns]], "KIQ"
        )
    if not bmx.empty:
        out = safe_merge(
            out, bmx[[c for c in ["SEQN", "BMXBMI"] if c in bmx.columns]], "BMX"
        )
    if not bpq.empty:
        out = safe_merge(
            out,
            bpq[[c for c in ["SEQN", "BPQ020", "BPQ080"] if c in bpq.columns]],
            "BPQ",
        )
    if not alq.empty:
        out = safe_merge(
            out, alq[[c for c in ["SEQN", "ALQ130"] if c in alq.columns]], "ALQ"
        )

    # Add demographics we need
    demo_keep = [
        c
        for c in ["SEQN", "RIDAGEYR", "RIAGENDR", "RIDRETH3", "WTMEC2YR"]
        if c in demo.columns
    ]
    out = safe_merge(out, demo[demo_keep], "DEMO")

    # Adult filter
    if ADULTS_ONLY and "RIDAGEYR" in out.columns:
        out = out[pd.to_numeric(out["RIDAGEYR"], errors="coerce") >= 18].copy()

    # Require at least one lab?
    a1c = pd.to_numeric(out.get("LBXGH"), errors="coerce")
    fpg = pd.to_numeric(out.get("LBXGLU"), errors="coerce")
    if REQUIRE_LAB_FOR_ROW:
        out = out.loc[(a1c.notna() | fpg.notna())].copy()

    # Prediabetes flag (Target)
    out["prediabetes_flag"] = (
        a1c.between(5.7, 6.4, inclusive="both")
        | fpg.between(100, 125, inclusive="both")
    ).astype("Int64")

    # Exclude diagnosed DM / meds now
    if EXCLUDE_DIAGNOSED:
        d_dm = out.get("DIQ010")
        d_ins = out.get("DIQ050")
        d_pil = out.get("DIQ070")

        def yes(s):
            return (
                s.map({1: 1, 2: 0, 3: 0, 7: np.nan, 9: np.nan})
                if s is not None
                else pd.Series(index=out.index, dtype="float64")
            )

        mask = (yes(d_dm) == 1) | (yes(d_ins) == 1) | (yes(d_pil) == 1)
        out = out.loc[~mask.fillna(False)].copy()

    # Choose weight (no cycle scaling for single release)
    if REQUIRE_FASTING_FOR_WEIGHT and "WTSAF2YR" in out.columns:
        w = pd.to_numeric(out["WTSAF2YR"], errors="coerce")
    else:
        w = pd.to_numeric(out.get("WTMEC2YR"), errors="coerce")
    out["weight"] = w

    # Rename columns to readable names
    present_map = {k: v for k, v in RENAME.items() if k in out.columns}
    out.rename(columns=present_map, inplace=True)

    # Add readable answers
    if "sex_code" in out.columns:
        out["sex"] = out["sex_code"].map(SEX_L)
    if "race_eth_code" in out.columns:
        out["race_eth"] = out["race_eth_code"].map(RIDRETH3_L)

    yn_vars = [
        "tried_lose_weight_past_year_code",
        "regular_periods_12mo_code",
        "ever_pregnant_code",
        "smoked_100_cigs_code",
        "dpq_poor_appetite_code",
        "dpq_feeling_down_code",
        "ever_asthma_code",
        "anemia_treatment_3mo_code",
        "ever_chf_code",
        "ever_heart_attack_code",
        "ever_stroke_code",
        "ever_thyroid_problem_code",
        "ever_liver_condition_code",
        "liver_fatty_code",
        "liver_fibrosis_code",
        "liver_cirrhosis_code",
        "liver_viral_hep_code",
        "liver_autoimmune_hep_code",
        "ever_weak_kidneys_code",
        "ever_high_bp_code",
        "ever_high_chol_code",
        "diq_diag_diabetes_code",
    ]
    for c in yn_vars:
        if c in out.columns:
            out[c.replace("_code", "_ans")] = out[c].map(YN)

    if "smoke_now_code" in out.columns:
        out["smoke_now_ans"] = out["smoke_now_code"].map(SMOKE_NOW)

    # Sliases for targets
    if "a1c_percent" in out.columns:
        out["A1c"] = pd.to_numeric(out["a1c_percent"], errors="coerce")
    if "fpg_mg_dl" in out.columns:
        out["FPG"] = pd.to_numeric(out["fpg_mg_dl"], errors="coerce")

    # Final tidy
    out["SEQN"] = pd.to_numeric(out["SEQN"], errors="coerce").astype("Int64")
    out["cycle"] = L

    # Snap tiny float dust to 0
    eps = 1e-12
    numcols = out.select_dtypes(include=["float", "int"]).columns
    out[numcols] = out[numcols].apply(pd.to_numeric, errors="coerce")
    out[numcols] = out[numcols].mask(out[numcols].abs() < eps, 0.0)

    # Column order
    order = [
        c
        for c in [
            "prediabetes_flag",
            "SEQN",
            "cycle",
            "weight",
            "age_years",
            "sex_code",
            "sex",
            "race_eth_code",
            "race_eth",
            "height_in",
            "weight_lb",
            "weight_1y_ago_lb",
            "bmi",
            "sleep_hours_weekdays",
            "sedentary_minutes",
            "drinks_per_day_12mo",
            "smoked_100_cigs_code",
            "smoked_100_cigs_ans",
            "smoke_now_code",
            "smoke_now_ans",
            "tried_lose_weight_past_year_code",
            "tried_lose_weight_past_year_ans",
            "regular_periods_12mo_code",
            "regular_periods_12mo_ans",
            "ever_pregnant_code",
            "ever_pregnant_ans",
            "dpq_poor_appetite_code",
            "dpq_poor_appetite_ans",
            "dpq_feeling_down_code",
            "dpq_feeling_down_ans",
            "ever_asthma_code",
            "ever_asthma_ans",
            "anemia_treatment_3mo_code",
            "anemia_treatment_3mo_ans",
            "ever_chf_code",
            "ever_chf_ans",
            "ever_heart_attack_code",
            "ever_heart_attack_ans",
            "ever_stroke_code",
            "ever_stroke_ans",
            "ever_thyroid_problem_code",
            "ever_thyroid_problem_ans",
            "ever_liver_condition_code",
            "ever_liver_condition_ans",
            "liver_fatty_code",
            "liver_fatty_ans",
            "liver_fibrosis_code",
            "liver_fibrosis_ans",
            "liver_cirrhosis_code",
            "liver_cirrhosis_ans",
            "liver_viral_hep_code",
            "liver_viral_hep_ans",
            "liver_autoimmune_hep_code",
            "liver_autoimmune_hep_ans",
            "ever_weak_kidneys_code",
            "ever_weak_kidneys_ans",
            "ever_high_bp_code",
            "ever_high_bp_ans",
            "ever_high_chol_code",
            "ever_high_chol_ans",
            "A1c",
            "FPG",
            "a1c_percent",
            "fpg_mg_dl",
            "weight_sample",
            "weight_fasting",
        ]
        if c in out.columns
    ]
    # append any remaining columns not listed
    order += [c for c in out.columns if c not in order]
    out = out[order]

    return out
 ```
```{python}
Path(os.path.dirname(OUT_CSV) or ".").mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
parts = [build_cycle(L) for L in CYCLES]
df = pd.concat(parts, ignore_index=True)

# Deduplicate any duplicate 'weight' columns and drop invalid weights
if (df.columns == "weight").sum() > 1:
    warnings.warn(f"Duplicate 'weight' columns detected ({(df.columns == 'weight').sum()}). Combining.")
    wcols = df.loc[:, df.columns == "weight"]
    w = wcols.bfill(axis=1).iloc[:, 0]
    df = df.drop(columns=[c for c in wcols.columns if c != wcols.columns[0]])
    df = df.rename(columns={wcols.columns[0]: "weight"})
    df["weight"] = pd.to_numeric(w, errors="coerce")
elif "weight" in df.columns:
    df["weight"] = pd.to_numeric(df["weight"], errors="coerce")

if "weight" in df.columns:
    before = len(df)
    df = df[df["weight"] > 0].copy()
    print(f"Dropped rows with invalid/nonpositive weight: {before - len(df)}")

df.to_csv(OUT_CSV, index=False)
print(f"Saved {OUT_CSV}  rows={len(df)}  cols={df.shape[1]}")
```
```{python}
import pandas as pd, numpy as np, os, json, math

IN_CSV = "data-raw/nhanes2123_prediabetes_features_labeled.csv"
OUT_CSV = "data-raw/nhanes2123_prediabetes_features_labeled_clean.csv"
OUT_DD = "data-raw/nhanes2123_prediabetes_features_datadict.json"

df = pd.read_csv(IN_CSV)

# Make sure SEQN is integer & unique
if "SEQN" in df.columns:
    df["SEQN"] = pd.to_numeric(df["SEQN"], errors="coerce").astype("Int64")
    if df["SEQN"].duplicated().any():
        df["_lab_count"] = (
            df[["A1c", "FPG"]].apply(lambda r: r.notna().sum(), axis=1)
            if {"A1c", "FPG"} <= set(df.columns)
            else 0
        )
        df = (
            df.sort_values(["SEQN", "_lab_count"], ascending=[True, False])
            .drop_duplicates(subset=["SEQN"], keep="first")
            .drop(columns=["_lab_count"])
        )
else:
    raise SystemExit("SEQN column is required.")

# Collapse duplicate 'weight' columns; drop nonpositive weights
wcols = [c for c in df.columns if c == "weight"]
if len(wcols) > 1:
    w = df[wcols].bfill(axis=1).iloc[:, 0]
    df = df.drop(columns=wcols).assign(weight=w)
elif len(wcols) == 1:
    df["weight"] = pd.to_numeric(df["weight"], errors="coerce")
else:
    # try to pick one from available sample weights
    for cand in ["weight_sample", "weight_fasting", "WTMEC2YR", "WTSAF2YR"]:
        if cand in df.columns:
            df["weight"] = pd.to_numeric(df[cand], errors="coerce")
            break

if "weight" in df.columns:
    df = df[df["weight"].fillna(0) > 0].copy()

# Coerce numerics & snap tiny float dust to 0
num_like = [
    "age_years",
    "bmi",
    "A1c",
    "FPG",
    "a1c_percent",
    "fpg_mg_dl",
    "sleep_hours_weekdays",
    "sedentary_minutes",
    "drinks_per_day_12mo",
    "height_in",
    "weight_lb",
    "weight_1y_ago_lb",
]
for c in num_like:
    if c in df.columns:
        df[c] = pd.to_numeric(df[c], errors="coerce")

eps = 1e-12
for c in df.select_dtypes(include=["float", "int"]).columns:
    df[c] = pd.to_numeric(df[c], errors="coerce")
    df[c] = df[c].mask(df[c].abs() < eps, 0.0)

# Range sanity (set out-of-bounds to NA)
ranges = {
    "age_years": (18, 99),
    "bmi": (10, 80),
    "A1c": (3, 20),
    "FPG": (50, 500),
    "sleep_hours_weekdays": (0, 24),
    "sedentary_minutes": (0, 1440),
    "drinks_per_day_12mo": (0, 50),
    "height_in": (40, 84),
    "weight_lb": (60, 700),
    "weight_1y_ago_lb": (60, 700),
}
for c, (lo, hi) in ranges.items():
    if c in df.columns:
        s = pd.to_numeric(df[c], errors="coerce")
        df.loc[(s < lo) | (s > hi), c] = np.nan

# Engineer a few helper features
# Computes BMI = weight(kg) / height(m)^2.
if {"height_in", "weight_lb"} <= set(df.columns):
    h_m = df["height_in"] * 0.0254
    w_kg = df["weight_lb"] * 0.45359237
    with np.errstate(invalid="ignore", divide="ignore"):
        df["bmi_from_selfreport"] = (w_kg / (h_m**2)).where((h_m > 0) & (w_kg > 0))

# Change in weight: current weight minus self-reported weight 1 year ago
# Positive: gained weight; Negative: lost weight
if {"weight_lb", "weight_1y_ago_lb"} <= set(df.columns):
    df["weight_change_1y_lb"] = df["weight_lb"] - df["weight_1y_ago_lb"]

# Maps Nhanes: Ever smoked 100+ cigarettes? to :
# Code 1(smoker): 1
# Code 2(never smoker): 0
# Code 7 (Refused), Code 9 (Dont know), missing: Nan
if "smoked_100_cigs_code" in df.columns:
    df["ever_smoker"] = df["smoked_100_cigs_code"].map({1: 1, 2: 0}).astype("float")

# Maps Nhanes: Do you smoke now? to:
# Code 1 (Every day) : 1
# Code 2 (Some days) : 1
# Code 3 (Not at all) : 0
# Code 7 (Refused), Code 9 (Dont know), Missing : Nan
if "smoke_now_code" in df.columns:
    df["current_smoker"] = df["smoke_now_code"].map({1: 1, 2: 1, 3: 0}).astype("float")

# Drop weight columns, cycle, Nan columns, and sex/race code columns, and columns used to construct prediabetes target or remove respondents with diabetes
df = df.drop(
    columns=[
        "cycle",
        "weight",
        "weight_sample",
        "weight_fasting",
        "sex_code",
        "race_eth_code",
        "ever_pregnant_code",
        "ever_pregnant_ans",
        "A1c",
        "FPG",
        "a1c_percent",
        "fpg_mg_dl",
        "diq_diag_diabetes_code",
        "diq_now_insulin_code",
        "diq_now_pills_code",
        "diq_diag_diabetes_ans",
    ],
    errors="ignore",
)

# Write cleaned CSV
os.makedirs(os.path.dirname(OUT_CSV), exist_ok=True)
df.to_csv(OUT_CSV, index=False)

#  Data dictionary
desc = {
    "SEQN": "Respondent sequence number (unique within release)",
    "weight": "Analysis weight (MEC default; fasting weight if chosen)",
    "A1c": "Glycohemoglobin (%)",
    "FPG": "Fasting plasma glucose (mg/dL)",
    "prediabetes_flag": "1 if A1c 5.7–6.4% or FPG 100–125 mg/dL",
    "age_years": "Age in years at screening",
    "sex_code / sex": "1=Male, 2=Female / labeled",
    "race_eth_code / race_eth": "RIDRETH3 code / labeled",
    "bmi": "Measured BMI (if present)",
    "bmi_from_selfreport": "BMI from self-reported height/weight (derived)",
    "weight_change_1y_lb": "Current weight - weight 1y ago (lb)",
    "ever_smoker": "1 if smoked ≥100 cigarettes in lifetime (SMQ020)",
    "current_smoker": "1 if smoke now every/some days (SMQ040)",
}
with open(OUT_DD, "w") as f:
    json.dump(desc, f, indent=2)


print("Saved:", OUT_CSV)
print("Data dictionary:", OUT_DD)

```



# Data description

Have an initial draft of your data description section. Your data description should be about your analysis-ready data.

# Data limitations

Identify any potential problems with your dataset.

# Exploratory data analysis

Perform an (initial) exploratory data analysis.

# Questions for reviewers

List specific questions for your peer reviewers and project mentor to answer in giving you feedback on this phase.
